title: DFU - Disconnecting/connecting earbuds to check DFU Pop Up
scenario: Pop-up "Update currently not available, please try back later" appears when one earbud is disconnected during firmware update, persists until dismissed on Software Update screen
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Navigate to Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth audio device (e.g., Poly Voyager Free 20) is connected.
3. Go to Device Settings → Software Update and initiate a firmware update.
4. During the firmware update process, remove one earbud (e.g., right earbud) from the charging case and place it back into the case to disconnect it.
5. Observe the pop-up message displayed on the app screen.
6. Verify that the pop-up message reads: "Update currently not available, please try back later".
7. Reconnect the earbud by removing it from the charging case.
8. Verify that the pop-up message remains on screen and is not automatically dismissed.
9. Tap the OK button on the pop-up message.
10. Verify that the user is returned to the Software Update screen.
11. Repeat steps 3–10 for the left earbud individually.
expected: When one earbud is disconnected during a firmware update, a pop-up message "Update currently not available, please try back later" appears. After reconnecting the earbud, the pop-up remains until dismissed by tapping OK, which returns the user to the Software Update screen. This behavior is consistent for both right and left earbuds individually.
